recent_messages.text: היא שוב שכחה לקחת את המעיל שלה מהגן. את יכולה לשים אותו בתיק של הערב?
כן, אני אשים. גם יש לה חוג אחרי זה?

notes: This is family logistics about a child’s belongings and schedule, even though the school/גן context could feel like home coordination.

recent_messages.text: אבא שלך ביקש שנעבור אליו בשישי, אבל הוא צריך עזרה לסדר את המרפסת לפני כולם מגיעים.
בטח, אני אביא גם את הכיסאות מהמחסן.

notes: The main topic is coordinating with a parent and relatives for a family gathering; the storage/porch detail is secondary.

recent_messages.text: Can you send me the latest wording for the intro? I want to make sure it matches what we promised.
Yes, I’ll clean it up and send it in ten.

notes: This is professional writing/editing coordination. It is work-related without using obvious keywords like meeting or project.

recent_messages.text: יש מצב שתסתכל על זה לפני שאני מעביר הלאה? יש שם שתי נקודות שעדיין לא יושבות טוב.
כן, תשלח עכשיו ואני אעבור.

notes: Reviewing something before passing it on is professional communication; the wording is generic but clearly work-oriented.

recent_messages.text: The sink is dripping again and the cabinet under it is getting wet.
I’ll buy a new seal on the way back and fix it tonight.

notes: This is about a household repair and maintenance issue in the apartment, so home is the best label.

recent_messages.text: החשבון של החשמל הגיע גבוה שוב. אולי נבדוק למה המזגן עובד כל הזמן?
כן, אני אעבור על זה כשאגיע.

notes: The focus is a utility bill and home energy use, which are household logistics rather than family relationship matters.

recent_messages.text: Are you still coming to the show tonight? We can grab something nearby first.
Yep, I’m in. Text me when you leave.

notes: This is a casual social plan with no family, work, or home responsibilities.

recent_messages.text: ראית את הסרטון ששלחתי? זה באמת מצחיק.
עוד לא, אני אראה אחרי הארוחה.

notes: Sharing a funny video is casual conversation and does not fit family, work, or home.

recent_messages.text: Did you hear from Uncle Avi about Saturday? He’s asking who is bringing dessert.
I’ll bring something sweet and text him later.

notes: The conversation is about organizing a family gathering with a relative.

recent_messages.text: אפשר שתאסוף את סבתא בדרך? היא לא רוצה לנסוע לבד בערב.
כן, אני אעבור אצלה אחרי העבודה.

notes: This is family coordination involving a grandmother, even though it mentions travel logistics.

recent_messages.text: Did you get a chance to review the draft I sent? I need your notes before I reply.
I’m on it now, will send comments in a bit.

notes: This is professional review and feedback on a draft, which fits work.

recent_messages.text: Can you cover the afternoon slot tomorrow? I have to finish the onboarding forms.
Yes, I can take it if you send me the handoff details.

notes: The message is about covering a shift and onboarding forms, both work-related.

recent_messages.text: The key is stuck again and the front door won’t latch properly.
I’ll call someone to fix it today.

notes: This is about a home repair/maintenance issue with the door lock.

recent_messages.text: Reminder: the elevator inspection is tomorrow morning, so it may stop for a while.
Thanks, I’ll plan to use the stairs.

notes: Building maintenance and elevator inspection are property/home logistics.

recent_messages.text: Are you still coming to the show tonight? I found an extra ticket.
Yeah, I’m in. Save it for me.

notes: This is a casual social plan about a show, with no family, work, or home topic.

recent_messages.text: שלחתי לך את הפודקאסט שדיברנו עליו, תגיד אם הוא טוב.
סגור, אאזין בדרך.

notes: Sharing a podcast is casual conversation and does not fit family, work, or home.

recent_messages.text: Did you hear from the school? They said the form has to go back today.
Yes, I signed it and left it on the table.

notes: This is family logistics around a school form, with the focus on coordinating at home for a household member.

recent_messages.text: Can you send me the latest version before I leave? I need to check the figures.
Sure, I’m fixing one slide now and then I’ll forward it.

notes: The conversation is about reviewing and sending work material; the tone is professional even without explicit work keywords.

recent_messages.text: The water heater is making a weird noise again.
I’ll call someone tomorrow morning to check it.

notes: This is about a home appliance/maintenance issue in a property, so home is the best label.

recent_messages.text: Are you still up for sushi later, or should we do it another night?
Tonight works for me.

notes: This is a casual social plan with no family, work, or household context.
